claim: OSM Portugal has an organized community subproject structure covering road hierarchies, hydrological features, protected areas, and administrative divisions with Faro (Algarve) as a named community node.
stance_target: OSM as viable base layer for Algarve territorial intelligence
scope_conditions: Portugal OSM wiki, current as of 2026
quoted_support: Subprojetos: Rodovias (Rede · Serviços · Percursos · Infraestruturas · Sinalização) ... Hidrologia (Águas Balneares · Albufeiras) · Áreas protegidas · Percursos pedestres

claim: The Portuguese OSM road classification uses a six-tier hierarchy: AE (autoestrada), IP (itinerário principal), IC (itinerário complementar), EN (estrada nacional), ER (estrada regional), CM (caminho municipal).
stance_target: road network encoding for Algarve territorial layer
quoted_support: Rodovias (AE · IP · IC · EN · ER · CM)

claim: OSM Portugal tracks hydrological features including albufeiras (reservoirs), barragens (dams), praias (beaches), and rios (rivers) under a dedicated Hidrologia subproject.
stance_target: water source data availability for lost dog movement analysis
quoted_support: Hidrologia: Parques · Reservas · Albufeiras · Barragens · Praias · Rios
